{"step_by_step_thinking": "The documents consistently indicate that sickle cell anemia is caused by a mutation in the beta-globin gene. Specifically, a single nucleotide change (point mutation) replaces glutamic acid with valine at position 6 of the beta-globin chain. This is clearly described in multiple sources, such as Document [3], Document [4], and Document [10]. There is no mention of gene deletion or modification, nor frame shift mutation.", "answer_choice": "C"}
